attacking_style: Pure sprint finisher launching at 200-300m, often earlier than rivals but sustaining speed through the line. No traditional attacking; relies on leadout train positioning and explosive final kick.
team_tactics: Completely dependent on structured leadout train (Deceuninck/Quick-Step textbook positioning, Astana struggles). Morkov crucial for energy conservation and final pilot. Team controls crosswinds and positions Cavendish front-row entering final kilometer.
terrain_preference: Pan-flat bunch sprints with long, straight finales. Survives technical circuits and crosswinds when protected. Catastrophically exposed on even gentle climbs (4% gradients) and wet technical finishes with tight corners.
vulnerability: Dropped first among sprinters on any categorized climb. Crash-prone in chaotic finishes, especially wet conditions or when forcing gaps. Vulnerable when boxed in final kilometer without team priority. Inferior positioning when leadout structure collapses.
key_patterns: Launches sprint 250-300m out, earlier than modern rivals, but peak power endurance sustains speed (3 observations)
First sprinter eliminated on climbs, loses minutes on 4% gradients with 10km+ remaining (3 observations)
Multiple crashes in technical/wet finishes when forcing position rather than patient following (5 crash incidents)
Completely reliant on leadout train quality: dominant with Quick-Step, anonymous with Astana infrastructure (7 observations)
Shows tactical opportunism following rival wheels when own leadout fails, e.g., Milan in Rome (2 observations)
Age-related timing disadvantage emerging: multiple 2nd/3rd places despite strong legs suggest diminished snap (4 observations)
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 Tour abandon after crashes and consistent 2nd-4th places despite presence suggest declining finishing speed and handling vulnerability at 38
